Although this site is considered to have "normal" planting style, the triangular part of the site has spaced out trees while the trees planted along the forest are microforest style (ie "hybrid" planting style)

Since there were no appropriate "control" sites on campus that were comparable to the planting site, we used nearby Parc Trenholme as a control site (also on de Maisonneuve, next to open grassy field, has mature trees)
This is the experimental microforest planted by Soverdi (see Jackie/Edith for details on what makes it experimental - something about soil...?)
This was set up a few days after its pair due to time constraints - will need to correct for this when processing ARU data
Even though this is UDEM-1, it's actually the control site for the UdeM site (my bad!) - ARU was configured as UDEM-1. Keep this in mind when analyzing data.
This is the planted site for UdeM. These coordinates were taken from Google Maps (GPS died) - retake coords and take elevation with Garmin next time on site!
Even though this is HRDP-2, it's actually the planting site for the HRDP site (my bad!) - ARU was configured as HRDP-2. Keep this in mind when analyzing data. GPS coordinates are reversed in Garmin.
This is the control site for HRDP.

This site is a mix of Soverdi projects (Carrefour, Sacré-Coeur, Soeurs-de-la-Providence) - need to consider that when looking at date of trees planted, etc. Also consider that this site is really large! Encompasses many buildings and likely different owners throughout the full extent of the site.
